response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned TypeScript/Node.js engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows. He has no discernible capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain experience; his one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was built entirely on TypeScript and GraphQL. There is no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, STL, Python, or relevant messaging frameworks, and his primary technical identity is web and platform engineering, not systems-level financial software.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development applied consistently across multiple roles
SQL listed as a skill with database experience across multiple projects and stacks
Demonstrated communication ability through public conference talks and multi-day technical workshops
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) versus the 5–10 years mandated by the role
Zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any relevant financial domain
Entire professional career is web-centric (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js); no Windows platform, SQL Server/Oracle, STL, or financial middleware experience
